intent: A high-performance e-commerce engine designed to reduce checkout abandonment through a unified payment gateway orchestration layer. The platform automates multi-currency conversion, handles real-time tax compliance, and uses a headless architecture to allow merchants to deploy instant purchase experiences across web, mobile, and social media channels.

entities.name: PaymentOrchestrator
entities.description: A centralized routing service that abstracts multiple third-party payment providers, managing transaction state transitions and handling multi-currency routing logic.

entities.name: CartReservationState
entities.description: An ephemeral data structure tracking items in a transient state, managed via a distributed cache to ensure thread-safe inventory locking during the checkout flow.

entities.name: TaxComplianceSnapshot
entities.description: A ledger record capturing geo-specific tax rates, nexus rules, and regulatory calculation results at the precise moment of checkout initiation.

entities.name: CurrencyExchangeBuffer
entities.description: A high-frequency cache component providing near real-time FX rate conversions to ensure consistent pricing presentation across global headless channels.

entities.name: InventoryReservationEvent
entities.description: An immutable asynchronous message sent across the Kafka backbone to synchronize inventory levels across distributed stores immediately upon checkout progression.

entities.name: TransactionSettlementAudit
entities.description: A write-once ledger service that maintains a tamper-proof record of all finalized payments, reconciliation statuses, and webhook responses from external gateways.

architecture.description: The architecture follows an event-driven microservices pattern. Upon a user initiating checkout, the CartReservationState acquires a distributed lock on inventory. Simultaneously, the TaxComplianceSnapshot service fetches local regulatory data while the CurrencyExchangeBuffer determines the transaction amount in the merchant base currency. Once valid, the PaymentOrchestrator routes the secure tokenized request to the appropriate gateway (Stripe/PayPal) via PCI-DSS compliant channels. Upon successful authorization, an InventoryReservationEvent is published to Kafka, triggering downstream stock adjustments in the ERP. Finalization is confirmed by the TransactionSettlementAudit, which updates the ledger asynchronously, ensuring high performance by decoupling the transaction flow from complex backend inventory processing.

endpoints.summary: Create a new cart reservation and acquire inventory lock
Retrieve tax compliance snapshot for a specific reservation
Get real-time exchange rate for a currency
Route payment request to provider via orchestrator
Check status of inventory reservation event

rules.description: Maintain inventory lock consistency during cart reservation
rules.logic: When a new row is inserted into cart_reservation_states, the system must set lock_status to 'LOCKED'. If the process fails to complete within 500ms, the record in cart_reservation_states must be deleted.

rules.description: Validate tax compliance snapshot before payment processing
rules.logic: The payment_orchestrators record requires a corresponding entry in tax_compliance_snapshots linked by reservation_id before processing can proceed.
The currency_exchange_buffers.last_updated timestamp must be within 60 seconds of the current epoch time. If stale, the system must refresh the rate_to_base value before returning data.
A record in inventory_reservation_events is created only if the associated payment_orchestrators record for the same reservation_id has a state of 'Authorized'.

rules.description: Maintain transaction settlement audit ledger integrity
rules.logic: The audit_id in transaction_settlement_audits must remain immutable and unique. Attempts to insert duplicate audit records or modify existing records must be rejected.
